acceptance_targets: [chapter_10-prose-001] Pass if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:73, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:157, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:421, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:455, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:467 read in complete sentences without article-stripped corrections such as "Not X. Y." or equivalent clipped noun phrases; fail if three or more clipped correction beats still remain across those passages.

[chapter_10-craft-001] Pass if the revised span anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:455, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:467, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:503, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:519 progresses through distinct observable cues and names the "too perfect / repeatable" realization directly no more than once; fail if three separate paragraphs in that passage still restate the same conclusion in new synonyms.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:73, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:157, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:421, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:455, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:467 read in complete sentences without article-stripped corrections such as "Not X. Y." or equivalent clipped noun phrases; fail if three or more clipped correction beats still remain across those passages.
findings.problem: The narration repeatedly falls into clipped correction fragments such as "Not cruel. Worse. Tired." and "Not accusation. Inventory." This violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping and turns a once-useful emphasis beat into a chapter-wide tic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the clipped beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:73, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:157, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:421, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:455, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:467 into grammatically complete sentences that keep the pressure without dropping required function words. If one emphatic fragment remains, isolate it instead of repeating the "Not X. Y." pattern across those lines.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised span anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:455, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:467, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:503, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:519 progresses through distinct observable cues and names the "too perfect / repeatable" realization directly no more than once; fail if three separate paragraphs in that passage still restate the same conclusion in new synonyms.
findings.problem: The late Eli scene lands the first sensory chill, but after the first clean hit the chapter keeps explaining the same discovery in slightly different language. The repeated analytic paraphrase about familiarity, fitting, and repeatability reduces unease by translating the effect for the reader instead of letting each new beat deepen it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the suspicion sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:455, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:467, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:503, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:519 so each paragraph adds a distinct new cue or consequence rather than another restatement of the same thesis. Preserve the first moment of recognition, then let later beats show the seam through Eli's timing, recycled phrasing, or Lena's bodily reaction instead of repeated interpretive gloss.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
